assumptions: The declared dividend has created an intragroup monetary receivable and corresponding payable.
The question is asked from the perspective of consolidated financial statements.
The foreign currency denomination of the intragroup balance creates exchange differences relevant to consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities, or components of them, exposed to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including qualifying intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from net investments included in consolidated financial statements.
